reviews.review_title: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ A Hidden Gem for VR Strategy Fans!
reviews.review_description:
I’ve played a lot of VR games, but Age of Kingdoms VR surprised me in the best way possible. If you’ve ever wanted to step inside a medieval city builder instead of just looking at it from a screen, this game absolutely delivers.

Building your kingdom by physically placing structures with your own hands feels incredibly immersive. Watching villagers go about their daily lives, managing resources, expanding your settlement, and preparing defenses against raids makes you feel like a real ruler rather than someone clicking through menus.

What I enjoyed most is how much depth there is. It’s not just about placing buildings—you need to manage food, water, jobs, happiness, military forces, and production chains. Turning wheat into bread or producing beer for your taverns adds a satisfying layer of strategy, while enemy attacks keep you on your toes.

The VR controls feel natural, and it’s clear the game was designed specifically for VR rather than being a flat-screen game ported over. The sense of scale when looking over your growing kingdom is amazing.

If you’re a fan of city builders, management games, medieval settings, or strategy games in general, I highly recommend giving this one a try. It deserves far more attention than it’s getting. Watching your small village grow into a thriving kingdom is incredibly rewarding, and the gameplay loop kept me coming back for “just one more upgrade” again and again.

Highly recommended. One of the most enjoyable and immersive kingdom-building experiences available on Quest. 👑🏰⚔️🌾

developer_response.body:
Thank you! 
I am still working to improve the game every day!

reviews.review_description:
I've played every city-builder on the market. I've micromanaged grain supplies in Anno, agonized over road layouts in Cities: Skylines, and survived countless siege waves in Stronghold. But nothing — absolutely nothing — prepared me for what it feels like to actually stand inside your own kingdom.

Age of Kingdoms VR doesn't just port a strategy game into virtual reality. It rebuilds the entire genre from scratch around the fact that your hands are the interface. You reach out, grab a farm blueprint from your build menu, and physically place it in a sun-drenched field. You watch your villagers trudge out to harvest wheat, haul it to the mill, and eventually deliver warm bread to hungry families. The resource chain — wheat to flour to bread, hops to ale, timber to planks to buildings — unfolds all around you at ground level, and it is mesmerizing.

"The moment a raiding party crested the hill and I sprinted to the barracks to dispatch my soldiers, heart pounding — I forgot entirely I was wearing a headset."
The combat escalates beautifully. Early waves are manageable with a handful of spearmen. But by the time mythical creatures start appearing alongside organized armies, you will be frantically juggling troop placement, wall reinforcement, and resource triage all at once. It is stressful in the best possible way — the kind of stress that makes victory feel earned.

Villager happiness is where the depth really shows. Neglect the tavern and morale crumbles. Forget the church and your people lose faith in your rule. Run dry on water and disease follows. Every building serves the ecosystem, and every ecosystem failure cascades visibly through the village you've built with your own hands — making it hurt in a way a flat screen never could.

developer_response.body:
Thanks! 
The game is still in development and more and more things will be added all the time, so stand by!
Thank you for your kind review that motivates me to work harder and make the game better.

reviews.review_title: Finally, a Real VR Strategy Game ⚔️
reviews.review_description:
Finally, a Real VR Strategy Game ⚔️

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Most strategy games don’t translate well to VR, but this one absolutely does. Building, managing resources, and defending your kingdom feels immersive and fun from start to finish. If you’re looking for a VR game with depth and replayability, this is a great choice.

reviews.review_title: Addictive Medieval Kingdom Builder in VR
reviews.review_description: I didn’t expect to get hooked this quickly. The combination of city building, resource management, and defending your kingdom works really well in VR. Watching your village grow from a few buildings into a busy medieval town is incredibly satisfying. If you enjoy strategy games, this is definitely worth playing. Great job by the developers!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your support!

reviews.review_title: Finally, a Strategy Game That Feels Good
reviews.review_description:
Really impressed by this game. Building and managing a kingdom with your own hands makes everything feel much more engaging than traditional strategy games. Plenty to do, easy to get immersed in, and the time fly by without noticing. Also villagers quest are so fun! Grate idea!

Definitely recommended for anyone who likes medieval or management games 👍🏻

developer_response.body:
Thank you! 👍🏻
Keep close, new updates with more things are coming!

reviews.review_title: So much more fun than i expected! ☺️
reviews.review_description:
I’m usually not a big strategy game player, but this completely pulled me in. I loved watching my little village grow, helping villagers, and organizing my kingdom while keeping everyone happy. The VR immersion makes everything feel so much more personal and enjoyable. Such a charming and surprisingly little addictive game!
Maybe you can make it in mixed reality also, it will be fun!

reviews.review_title: Really fun kingdom builder in vr
reviews.review_description:
As someone who loves strategy and management games, I honestly didn’t expect to get so hooked on Age of Kingdoms VR. From the moment I started building my first village, I was completely immersed. There is something incredibly satisfying about standing over your kingdom in VR, placing buildings with your own hands, watching villagers go about their daily lives, and seeing your small settlement grow into a powerful kingdom.
What makes this game special is how immersive everything feels. Instead of just clicking buttons on a screen, you’re actually part of the world. Managing resources, expanding your territory, planning your town layout, and protecting your people all feel much more engaging in VR. Every decision matters, and watching your kingdom prosper because of your choices is extremely rewarding.
The gameplay is easy to understand but has enough depth to keep things interesting for hours. I constantly found myself saying, “I’ll just build one more thing,” only to realize another hour had passed. The medieval atmosphere is charming, the mechanics are enjoyable, and the overall experience feels polished and relaxing while still offering strategic challenges.
As a woman who enjoys both cozy and strategy-focused games, I love how this game lets me be creative while also making me think and plan ahead. It’s the perfect balance between relaxing kingdom building and rewarding management gameplay.
If you’re looking for a VR game that offers creativity, strategy, progression, and a genuine sense of accomplishment, Age of Kingdoms VR is absolutely worth checking out. It’s one of those rare games that keeps pulling you back because there’s always something new to build, improve, or discover.
I can’t wait to see what future updates bring. Highly recommended!

I’ve played a lot of strategy games, and this one feels really unique in VR. Building and managing my kingdom is fun, the gameplay is easy to get into, and it’s surprisingly addictive. Definitely worth checking out if you enjoy city builders or strategy games.

I wasn’t expecting to get hooked this quickly. Building a kingdom in VR feels amazing, and watching your village grow around you is incredibly satisfying. The gameplay is easy to learn but keeps you coming back for more. If you enjoy strategy games, this is definitely one you should try.
Nice one devs!

developer_response.body:
Thanks! Keep close, new updates are coming!
Cheers!

reviews.review_description: Only spent about 20 minutes in the game, but it left a good impression. I mainly wanted to see how the building worked, and before I knew it I was placing structures and checking on my villagers. Everything feels smooth and easy to pick up. Definitely seems like the kind of game you can lose track of time in once your kingdom starts growing.

Didn’t expect to get hooked this fast. Building and managing a kingdom in VR feels amazing, and watching your village grow while defending it from attacks is incredibly satisfying. If you enjoy strategy or city-building games, Age of Kingdoms VR is definitely worth picking up.🔝

reviews.review_title: The game is on the right path
reviews.review_description:
I think the game is very good. I hope the developers will improve it more in time but is a good game.
I will like to add monsters and maybe some events to defend against them. 
Keep the good job going!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and also for the ideas. I noted them and I will take in consideration for a future update. Thanks!
